notes: Thin cell: the smallest round-trip (one user prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use) whose ONLY extra assertion over basic-turn is that the daemon extracted a model_name. codex boots on a concrete model and writes it into each turn's turn_context.payload.model; extractCodexMetadata (parser.go:263-278) reads it and sets ev.ModelName, and the tailer applies it via applyModelMetadata even though turn_context is Skip=true — this is the path that issue #361 (codex dropping the model on token_count events) regressed and that is now fixed. The model_name assertion is NOT expressible in the events.jsonl phase DSL (the validator Phase struct carries no model field), so it lives at summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden, which replay-fixtures.sh diffs. Per issue #440 the concrete model string is environment-dependent (whatever model the account boots on), so the assertion is that a model_name IS present (non-empty, not 'unknown'), NOT a hardcoded value — keeping the cell deterministic and worktree-portable. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver, not headless `codex exec`: a single-shot exec turn is too short for the daemon to observe the ready→working flip (debounce coalesces it; the process exits first). The daemon sees proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then one working/ready flip, then process_exited at REPL teardown. first_turn_end anchors to first_turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready. Assert model/context only AFTER turn_end (ContextWindow may briefly read 0 between session detection and the first turn_context/task_started event).

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt → working on the rollout session
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName is the launch model (not 'unknown'), evidenced at summary.model_name in the replay golden; state never entered waiting
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
